title: HPOM-006: Release gate — distinguish APPROVED from RELEASED
description: APPROVED means review passed. RELEASED means work is integrated into active project state. Add RELEASED as a valid task status in the SQLite schema. Create scripts/release_task.py with a release checklist: shared-file updates, decisions recorded, follow-up tasks created, event log entry. A task cannot become RELEASED without completing the checklist. Update map_task.py to support 'release' subcommand.
objective: Add a RELEASED terminal status distinct from APPROVED. Implement release_task.py with a pre-release checklist gate. Add map_task.py release subcommand. No task may move APPROVED→RELEASED without a completed checklist record.
forbidden_changes: Do not modify task claiming logic in db/claims.py; do not change APPROVED or SUBMITTED status semantics; do not remove existing map_task.py subcommands
expected_artifacts: MAP_System/scripts/release_task.py, updated MAP_System/scripts/map_task.py, updated schema if needed, test evidence
risk: LOW — additive new status; existing approve/reject/claim paths are unchanged
acceptance_criteria: RELEASED status exists in SQLite schema
release_task.py enforces checklist before marking RELEASED
tasks cannot go APPROVED -> RELEASED without a checklist completion record
